title: tulophos laxa
intro: Tulophos laxa, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. It is native to Europe and Asia, and its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Tulophos laxa, including tinctures and infusions, can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall skin health.
